Description: Marginal cost for transmission lines in Cost-Benefit Analysis networks. Used to represent congestion costs and market inefficiencies. Defaults to 0.01.
List of planning horizons for which to run CBA
CBA methodologies to apply. Options include 'toot' (Take One Out at a Time) and 'pint' (Put In at a Time). Defaults to ['toot', 'pint'].
List of TYNDP project identifiers to evaluate in the CBA workflow. Format follows TYNDP project naming conventions (e.g., 't1-t35').

Mode for determining TOOT/PINT method assignment per project. 'entsoe_labels' extracts from Excel 'Chosen Approach' column (default), 'custom_labels' reads from existing method_assignment.csv, 'algorithmic' uses heuristic rules (not yet implemented).

List of store carrier names that should remain cyclic during CBA dispatch (e.g., battery, home battery, and fuel stores like lignite, coal, oil, uranium). These maintain e_cyclic=True in simplify_sb_network.

List of store carrier names with seasonal behavior (e.g., H2 Store, gas, co2 sequestered). These have e_cyclic=False and receive marginal storage values (MSV) from the perfect foresight MSV extraction to capture seasonal patterns in rolling horizon optimization.
Temporal resolution for MSV extraction solve (e.g., '3H', '6H', '24H'). Lower resolution reduces solve time while still capturing seasonal patterns. Set to false for full hourly resolution.
Threshold for warning about extreme MSV values. Values above this threshold may indicate model issues or unusual scarcity patterns. Default is 1000.

Method for resampling MSV to target network resolution. 'ffill' (forward fill, default) is appropriate for shadow prices representing discrete time periods. 'interpolate' provides smoother transitions. 'rolling_mean' applies smoothing after forward fill (requires rolling_window).
Window size (number of snapshots) for rolling mean smoothing. Only used when resample_method='rolling_mean'. Set to null for other methods.

Configuration options specific to CBA network optimization using rolling horizon approach. Uses the same structure as the top-level ``solving`` section (see :ref:`solving_cf`) with CBA-specific defaults for ``options: horizon`` (168 hours) and ``solver: name`` (highs).
